title: X-Basis Noise Immunity — Cross-Backend Replication (ORQ #1)
open_research_question: Does X-basis immunity (Finding 03) generalize across the heavy-hex family? Pre-reg gate: >=2x X/Z fidelity ratio on an independent backend.

marrakesh_reference.note: Finding 03 Bell C3650: <XX> immune (flat), <ZZ> gamma~1.197 accel, <YY> gamma~0.707; ZZ/XX err ratio ~3x

preregistered_criteria.T1: eZZ/eXX >= 2.0 AND eXX < eZZ  [headline: README >=2x gate]
preregistered_criteria.T2: eYY > eXX with eYY-eXX >= 0.02  [S-dagger injection signature]
preregistered_criteria.T3: gamma_ZZ > gamma_XX  [Z-basis scales steeper under ZNE]
preregistered_criteria.T1_FAIL: X-basis immunity is marrakesh substrate-specific, not architectural

summary.verdict: INCONCLUSIVE / CONFOUNDED — do NOT downgrade Finding 03. The pre-reg T1 gate technically FAILS (ZZ/XX ratio 0.79x, no X-immunity, ordering inverts), BUT the run is confounded by a gate-count-INDEPENDENT ~20-26pp error floor that is (a) inconsistent with the excellent reported calibration (cz_0_1 err=0.16%, readout ~1%, T2~180us — which predicts <ZZ> >=0.95) and (b) FLAT across ZNE folding lambda=1/3/5 (ZZ 0.795->0.803, even rising). Flatness-across-folds proves the error is NOT CZ-gate-driven — it is a constant SPAM/layout/transient floor. Finding 03's X-basis mechanism is a GATE-noise commutation effect; when gate noise is swamped by a non-gate floor, the asymmetry cannot appear regardless of whether the mechanism is real. So this neither confirms nor refutes the architectural claim. Provisional read: X-basis immunity is CONTINGENT on Z-dephasing being the DOMINANT channel, which did not hold in this kingston run. RETEST REQUIRED (Exp32): pin initial_layout to a verified best pair, add a lambda=1 raw-fidelity sanity gate (require baseline <ZZ> >= 0.90 before trusting the asymmetry test), optionally add ibm_fez as a 3rd device.
summary.confound_note: error floor flat across folding => not gate-driven; inconsistent with cz_0_1=0.16%/readout~1% calibration => SPAM/layout/transient floor swamps test
